chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 3374 of 2024 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.10691/2025. 2. For orders on CMA No.14912/2025. 3. For hearing of main case. 27.05.2025 Mr. Akbar Hussain Zuberi, advocate for the petitioner. 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. The prayer clause reads as follows: “1. Direct all the Associations which are working in University of Karachi not to interfere in the working and administration of the university and to immediately de-seat all the officials of the welfare organizations from all the committees of University of Karachi and not to pressurize the administrative staff of the university administration and to desist from using blackmailing tactics. 2. Direct the Registrar of Social Welfare Dept. Govt. of Sindh to cancel the registration of all the Associations who are not working in accordance to their mandate and constitution. And not to grant any registration to any welfare association who are affiliated to any political party. 3. Direct the Director Finance to remove all objections as pointed in the Audit Report of Auditor General Sindh. 4. That all these Associations who are registered as Welfare Associations should be directed not to exceed their mandate and work against their approval constitution and there should be only one Collective Bargaining Agent to look after the affairs of the employees in accordance with the standing orders of employees relations. 5.

Direct the Director Finance to remove all objections as pointed in the Audit Report of Auditor General Sindh. 4. That all these Associations who are registered as Welfare Associations should be directed not to exceed their mandate and work against their approval constitution and there should be only one Collective Bargaining Agent to look after the affairs of the employees in accordance with the standing orders of employees relations. 5. Any other relief that this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the interest of justice and to save University of Karachi from financial crunch and ultimate disaster. 6. That more verbal argument will be presented with the permission of this Hon’ble Court at the time of hearing of the Petition.” Counsel was confronted as to locus standi. Counsel was further sought to assist as to how writ can be issued against an entity other than that that contemplated by Article 199 of the Constitution and once again remains unable to assist. Petition is dismissed in limine alongwith pending application/s. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
